prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene that explains a probability-style bell curve with a moving camera, a tracked cutoff point, shaded area, and a small dashboard table that changes focus from tail to center mass.
completion:
from manim import *

class CameraProbabilityCurveDashboardVariation(MovingCameraScene):
    def construct(self):
        def density(x):
            return 1.35 * np.exp(-((x - 0.5) ** 2) / 1.6)

        title = Text("Tail Region Versus Central Mass", font_size=35).to_edge(UP)
        axes = Axes(x_range=[-3, 4, 1], y_range=[0, 1.8, 0.3], x_length=8.4, y_length=4.7, tips=False).shift(DOWN * 0.25)
        graph = axes.plot(density, x_range=[-2.8, 3.8], color=BLUE_D)
        cutoff = ValueTracker(2.2)
        probe = always_redraw(lambda: Dot(axes.c2p(cutoff.get_value(), density(cutoff.get_value())), color=YELLOW, radius=0.08))
        vline = always_redraw(lambda: DashedLine(axes.c2p(cutoff.get_value(), 0), probe.get_center(), color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=3))
        tail_area = always_redraw(lambda: axes.get_area(graph, x_range=(cutoff.get_value(), 3.6), color=ORANGE, opacity=0.28))
        center_area = always_redraw(lambda: axes.get_area(graph, x_range=(-0.5, 1.5), color=TEAL_D, opacity=0.22))
        table = MathTable([["region", "role"], ["tail", "rare outcomes"], ["center", "common mass"], ["zoom", "local detail"]], include_outer_lines=True).scale(0.4).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(DOWN * 0.15)
        row_tail = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[1], color=ORANGE, buff=0.05)
        row_center = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[2], color=TEAL_D, buff=0.05)
        row_zoom = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[3], color=PURPLE_D, buff=0.05)
        caption_a = Text("The tail isolates unlikely but important outcomes.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("The center holds most of the mass under the curve.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("A camera zoom makes the local slope and area boundary easier to inspect.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(axes), Create(graph), FadeIn(table), run_time=4)
        self.play(FadeIn(tail_area), FadeIn(probe), Create(vline), Create(row_tail), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=4)
        self.play(self.camera.frame.animate.scale(0.7).move_to(probe.get_center()), cutoff.animate.set_value(1.9), run_time=4)
        self.play(FadeIn(center_area), ReplacementTransform(row_tail, row_center), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=4)
        self.play(cutoff.animate.set_value(0.9), run_time=4)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(row_center, row_zoom), ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), self.camera.frame.animate.scale(0.72).move_to(axes.c2p(0.9, density(0.9))), run_time=4)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene that shows a 3D surface with two different probe paths. Use a fixed-frame mini table to compare ridge motion versus valley motion while the camera rotates around the surface.
completion:
from manim import *

class SurfaceDualProbeVariation(ThreeDScene):
    def construct(self):
        def z_fn(u, v):
            return 0.32 * np.cos(u) + 0.22 * np.sin(v) + 0.08 * u * v

        title = Text("Different Paths Tell Different 3D Stories", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        table = MathTable([["path", "behavior"], ["ridge", "higher sweep"], ["valley", "lower sweep"]], include_outer_lines=True).scale(0.4).to_corner(UR).shift(DOWN * 0.6)
        caption_a = Text("A ridge path stays higher across the same surface.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("A valley path samples a very different local shape.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(title, table, caption_a, caption_b)
        caption_b.set_opacity(0)

        axes = ThreeDAxes(x_range=[-3, 3, 1], y_range=[-3, 3, 1], z_range=[-2, 2, 1], x_length=7, y_length=7, z_length=4.4)
        surface = Surface(lambda u, v: axes.c2p(u, v, z_fn(u, v)), u_range=[-3, 3], v_range=[-3, 3], resolution=(18, 18), checkerboard_colors=[BLUE_D, TEAL_D], fill_opacity=0.66)
        ridge = ParametricFunction(lambda t: axes.c2p(t, 1.4, z_fn(t, 1.4)), t_range=[-3, 3], color=YELLOW_D)
        valley = ParametricFunction(lambda t: axes.c2p(t, -1.3, z_fn(t, -1.3)), t_range=[-3, 3], color=RED_D)
        ridge_probe = Dot3D(axes.c2p(-3, 1.4, z_fn(-3, 1.4)), color=YELLOW, radius=0.07)
        valley_probe = Dot3D(axes.c2p(-3, -1.3, z_fn(-3, -1.3)), color=RED_D, radius=0.07)
        ridge_box = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[1], color=YELLOW_D, buff=0.05)
        valley_box = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[2], color=RED_D, buff=0.05)

        self.set_camera_orientation(phi=64 * DEGREES, theta=-52 * DEGREES)
        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(table), Create(axes), run_time=4)
        self.play(Create(surface), run_time=5)
        self.play(Create(ridge), FadeIn(ridge_probe), Create(ridge_box), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=4)
        self.begin_ambient_camera_rotation(rate=0.12)
        self.play(MoveAlongPath(ridge_probe, ridge), run_time=6, rate_func=linear)
        self.play(Create(valley), FadeIn(valley_probe), ReplacementTransform(ridge_box, valley_box), FadeOut(caption_a), FadeIn(caption_b), run_time=4)
        self.play(MoveAlongPath(valley_probe, valley), run_time=5, rate_func=linear)
        self.wait(2)
        self.stop_ambient_camera_rotation()

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene that combines a curve, a bar-style summary panel, and a compact table. The scene should walk through three regions of the graph and keep the bars and table aligned with the current explanation beat.
completion:
from manim import *

class GraphTableBarTransitionVariation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Summarizing a Curve with Multiple Views", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        axes = Axes(x_range=[0, 9, 1], y_range=[0, 10, 2], x_length=6.6, y_length=4.5, tips=False).shift(LEFT * 2.2 + DOWN * 0.15)
        graph = axes.plot(lambda x: 1.2 + 0.75 * x + 1.8 * np.exp(-((x - 5.0) ** 2) / 1.5), x_range=[0.4, 8.6], color=BLUE_D)
        marker_left = Dot(axes.c2p(2.0, 1.2 + 0.75 * 2.0 + 1.8 * np.exp(-((2.0 - 5.0) ** 2) / 1.5)), color=BLUE_D, radius=0.07)
        marker_mid = Dot(axes.c2p(5.0, 1.2 + 0.75 * 5.0 + 1.8 * np.exp(-((5.0 - 5.0) ** 2) / 1.5)), color=YELLOW_D, radius=0.07)
        marker_right = Dot(axes.c2p(7.6, 1.2 + 0.75 * 7.6 + 1.8 * np.exp(-((7.6 - 5.0) ** 2) / 1.5)), color=GREEN_D, radius=0.07)
        bars = BarChart(values=[3, 8, 6], bar_names=["left", "middle", "right"], y_range=[0, 10, 2], y_length=3.0, x_length=3.4, bar_colors=[BLUE_D, YELLOW_D, GREEN_D]).scale(0.75).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(UP * 1.0)
        table = MathTable([["region", "reading"], ["left", "steady rise"], ["middle", "peak influence"], ["right", "declining bump"]], include_outer_lines=True).scale(0.32).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(DOWN * 1.85)
        box_left = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[1], color=BLUE_D, buff=0.05)
        box_mid = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[2], color=YELLOW_D, buff=0.05)
        box_right = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[3], color=GREEN_D, buff=0.05)
        caption_a = Text("The left region mostly follows the baseline trend.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("The middle region gets an extra lift from the central bump.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("On the right, the bump fades and the curve settles back down.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(axes), Create(graph), FadeIn(bars), FadeIn(table), run_time=4)
        self.play(FadeIn(marker_left), Create(box_left), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=3)
        self.play(FadeIn(marker_mid), ReplacementTransform(box_left, box_mid), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), bars.animate.change_bar_values([3, 9, 6]), run_time=4)
        self.play(FadeIn(marker_right), ReplacementTransform(box_mid, box_right), ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), bars.animate.change_bar_values([3, 9, 5]), run_time=4)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene with stream lines, three horizontal flow bands, a moving probe path, and a compact table that names the band behavior. Keep the scene lightweight enough to render quickly while still being multi-beat.
completion:
from manim import *

class StreamlinesBandProbeVariation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Flow Bands and a Moving Probe", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        plane = NumberPlane(x_range=[-4, 4, 1], y_range=[-3, 3, 1], background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.3}).shift(LEFT * 2.5 + DOWN * 0.15)
        stream_lines = StreamLines(lambda pos: np.array([1.0 - 0.16 * pos[1], 0.12 * np.cos(pos[0]), 0.0]), x_range=[-4, 4, 1.0], y_range=[-3, 3, 1.0], stroke_width=2.0, max_anchors_per_line=14)
        top_band = Rectangle(width=4.6, height=1.2, stroke_color=BLUE_D, fill_color=BLUE_D, fill_opacity=0.08).move_to(plane.c2p(0, 1.8))
        mid_band = Rectangle(width=4.6, height=1.2, stroke_color=YELLOW_D, fill_color=YELLOW_D, fill_opacity=0.08).move_to(plane.c2p(0, 0.0))
        low_band = Rectangle(width=4.6, height=1.2, stroke_color=GREEN_D, fill_color=GREEN_D, fill_opacity=0.08).move_to(plane.c2p(0, -1.8))
        probe = Dot(plane.c2p(-2.4, 1.0), color=YELLOW, radius=0.08)
        path = VMobject(color=YELLOW_D)
        path.set_points_smoothly([plane.c2p(-2.4, 1.0), plane.c2p(-1.2, 0.5), plane.c2p(0.3, 0.0), plane.c2p(1.5, -0.6), plane.c2p(2.6, -1.0)])
        table = MathTable([["band", "behavior"], ["top", "slower"], ["middle", "balanced"], ["bottom", "faster"]], include_outer_lines=True).scale(0.34).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(DOWN * 0.2)
        row_top = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[1], color=BLUE_D, buff=0.05)
        row_mid = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[2], color=YELLOW_D, buff=0.05)
        row_low = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[3], color=GREEN_D, buff=0.05)
        caption_a = Text("The upper band moves more gently than the lower band.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("A probe path crosses the bands and feels those changing speeds.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(plane), FadeIn(table), run_time=3)
        self.add(stream_lines)
        stream_lines.start_animation(warm_up=False, flow_speed=1.2)
        self.play(Create(top_band), Create(row_top), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=3)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(top_band, mid_band), ReplacementTransform(row_top, row_mid), run_time=3)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(mid_band, low_band), ReplacementTransform(row_mid, row_low), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=3)
        self.play(FadeIn(probe), MoveAlongPath(probe, path), run_time=5, rate_func=linear)
        self.wait(4)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 30-second ZoomedScene that follows a curve up to a sharp peak, zooms into the peak region, and uses a small table to distinguish approach, peak, and release phases.
completion:
from manim import *

class ZoomedPeakTableVariation(ZoomedScene):
    def __init__(self, **kwargs):
        super().__init__(zoom_factor=0.4, zoomed_display_height=2.3, zoomed_display_width=4.0, image_frame_stroke_width=10, zoomed_camera_config={"default_frame_stroke_width": 3}, **kwargs)

    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Approach, Peak, and Release", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        axes = Axes(x_range=[0, 10, 1], y_range=[0, 9, 1], x_length=7.0, y_length=4.4, tips=False).shift(LEFT * 1.6 + DOWN * 0.2)
        graph = axes.plot(lambda x: 1.0 + 0.45 * x + 2.6 * np.exp(-((x - 6.0) ** 2) / 0.8), x_range=[0.5, 9.2], color=BLUE_D)
        tracker = ValueTracker(3.5)
        probe = always_redraw(lambda: Dot(axes.i2gp(tracker.get_value(), graph), color=YELLOW, radius=0.08))
        vline = always_redraw(lambda: DashedLine(axes.c2p(tracker.get_value(), 0), probe.get_center(), color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=3))
        table = MathTable([["phase", "reading"], ["approach", "rising slope"], ["peak", "local maximum"], ["release", "falling away"]], include_outer_lines=True).scale(0.34).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(DOWN * 0.2)
        row_a = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[1], color=BLUE_D, buff=0.05)
        row_b = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[2], color=YELLOW_D, buff=0.05)
        row_c = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[3], color=GREEN_D, buff=0.05)
        caption_a = Text("The curve climbs toward a concentrated peak region.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("A zoom window isolates the local maximum and nearby shape.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("After the peak, the curve drops back toward the broader trend.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        frame = self.zoomed_camera.frame
        display = self.zoomed_display
        display.shift(DOWN * 2.0)
        display.display_frame.set_color(RED_D)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(axes), Create(graph), FadeIn(table), run_time=4)
        self.play(FadeIn(probe), Create(vline), Create(row_a), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=3)
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(5.7), run_time=4)
        frame.move_to(probe)
        frame.set_color(PURPLE)
        self.play(Create(frame), ReplacementTransform(row_a, row_b), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=3)
        self.activate_zooming()
        self.play(self.get_zoomed_display_pop_out_animation(), run_time=3)
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(6.4), run_time=3)
        pop_in = self.get_zoomed_display_pop_out_animation()
        self.play(pop_in, ReplacementTransform(row_b, row_c), ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), rate_func=lambda t: smooth(1 - t), run_time=3)
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(8.0), run_time=3)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene that uses MovingCameraScene to walk through a bar chart dashboard. Highlight the baseline, growth, and evaluation bars one by one while a small table updates the current interpretation.
completion:
from manim import *

class MovingCameraBarChartFocusVariation(MovingCameraScene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Following a Chart with a Moving Camera", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        bars = BarChart(values=[2.5, 5.5, 4.0], bar_names=["baseline", "growth", "eval"], y_range=[0, 8, 1], y_length=4.1, x_length=5.2, bar_colors=[BLUE_D, TEAL_D, GREEN_D]).shift(LEFT * 1.7 + DOWN * 0.15)
        labels = bars.get_bar_labels(font_size=28)
        table = MathTable([["stage", "reading"], ["baseline", "starting point"], ["growth", "largest jump"], ["eval", "stability check"]], include_outer_lines=True).scale(0.34).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(DOWN * 0.2)
        row_a = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[1], color=BLUE_D, buff=0.05)
        row_b = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[2], color=TEAL_D, buff=0.05)
        row_c = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[3], color=GREEN_D, buff=0.05)
        caption_a = Text("The first bar anchors the story with a small baseline.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("The middle bar is where the main jump in performance appears.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("The final bar checks whether the improvement actually holds up.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        focus_a = SurroundingRectangle(VGroup(bars[0], labels[0]), color=BLUE_D, buff=0.18)
        focus_b = SurroundingRectangle(VGroup(bars[1], labels[1]), color=TEAL_D, buff=0.18)
        focus_c = SurroundingRectangle(VGroup(bars[2], labels[2]), color=GREEN_D, buff=0.18)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(bars), FadeIn(labels), FadeIn(table), run_time=4)
        self.play(Create(row_a), Create(focus_a), FadeIn(caption_a), self.camera.frame.animate.scale(0.82).move_to(focus_a), run_time=4)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(row_a, row_b), ReplacementTransform(focus_a, focus_b), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), bars.animate.change_bar_values([2.5, 6.6, 4.0]), self.camera.frame.animate.scale(0.85).move_to(focus_b), run_time=4)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(row_b, row_c), ReplacementTransform(focus_b, focus_c), ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), bars.animate.change_bar_values([2.5, 6.6, 4.8]), self.camera.frame.animate.scale(0.9).move_to(focus_c), run_time=4)
        self.play(self.camera.frame.animate.scale(1.55).move_to(VGroup(bars, table)), run_time=3)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene that explains a 2D flow with an ArrowVectorField, stream lines, and a moving probe. Use a compact table to distinguish left, center, and right flow behavior without relying on custom helper methods.
completion:
from manim import *

class ArrowVectorFieldProbeVariation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Reading a Flow Field with Arrows and a Probe", font_size=33).to_edge(UP)
        plane = NumberPlane(x_range=[-4, 4, 1], y_range=[-3, 3, 1], background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.25}).shift(LEFT * 2.2 + DOWN * 0.15)
        field = ArrowVectorField(lambda pos: np.array([0.9 - 0.18 * pos[1], 0.22 * np.sin(pos[0]), 0.0]), x_range=[-4, 4, 1], y_range=[-3, 3, 1], colors=[BLUE_D, TEAL_D, GREEN_D], vector_config={"stroke_width": 2, "max_tip_length_to_length_ratio": 0.24})
        stream_lines = StreamLines(lambda pos: np.array([0.9 - 0.18 * pos[1], 0.22 * np.sin(pos[0]), 0.0]), x_range=[-4, 4, 1], y_range=[-3, 3, 1], stroke_width=2.0, max_anchors_per_line=14)
        probe = Dot(plane.c2p(-2.8, 0.8), color=YELLOW, radius=0.08)
        path = VMobject(color=YELLOW_D)
        path.set_points_smoothly([plane.c2p(-2.8, 0.8), plane.c2p(-1.0, 0.5), plane.c2p(0.6, 0.0), plane.c2p(2.0, -0.4), plane.c2p(3.0, -0.8)])
        table = MathTable([["zone", "behavior"], ["left", "gentle lift"], ["center", "balanced drift"], ["right", "stronger push"]], include_outer_lines=True).scale(0.34).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(DOWN * 0.2)
        row_a = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[1], color=BLUE_D, buff=0.05)
        row_b = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[2], color=TEAL_D, buff=0.05)
        row_c = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[3], color=GREEN_D, buff=0.05)
        zone_a = Rectangle(width=2.1, height=5.1, stroke_color=BLUE_D, fill_color=BLUE_D, fill_opacity=0.06).move_to(plane.c2p(-2.7, 0))
        zone_b = Rectangle(width=2.1, height=5.1, stroke_color=TEAL_D, fill_color=TEAL_D, fill_opacity=0.06).move_to(plane.c2p(0.0, 0))
        zone_c = Rectangle(width=2.1, height=5.1, stroke_color=GREEN_D, fill_color=GREEN_D, fill_opacity=0.06).move_to(plane.c2p(2.7, 0))
        caption_a = Text("On the left, the flow still rises gently.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("Near the center, the horizontal drift dominates the motion.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("Farther right, the probe exits with a stronger push.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(plane), Create(field), FadeIn(table), run_time=4)
        self.add(stream_lines)
        stream_lines.start_animation(warm_up=False, flow_speed=1.2)
        self.play(Create(zone_a), Create(row_a), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=3)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(zone_a, zone_b), ReplacementTransform(row_a, row_b), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=3)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(zone_b, zone_c), ReplacementTransform(row_b, row_c), ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), run_time=3)
        self.play(FadeIn(probe), MoveAlongPath(probe, path), run_time=5, rate_func=linear)
        self.wait(2)
